content.problemStatement:
An array is called lovely if the sum of product of each adjacent pair of elements is odd.

More formally, the array SSS of size MMM is lovely if the value ‚àëi=1M‚àí1\sum_{i=1}^{M-1}‚àëi=1M‚àí1‚Äã (Si.Si+1)(S_i . S_{i+1})(Si‚Äã.Si+1‚Äã) is **odd**.

You are given an array AAA consisting of NNN positive integers. Find a permutation of array AAA which is lovely.

If multiple such permutations are possible, output any. If there is no lovely permutation, output `-1`.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The TTT test cases then follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.


Output Format
For each test case, if a lovely permutation of AAA is possible, print a single line containing NNN integers denoting the elements of any lovely permutation of AAA. Otherwise, print `-1`.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111: The sum of products of adjacent pair of elements of the given array is 1‚ãÖ2+2‚ãÖ3+3‚ãÖ4+4‚ãÖ10=2+6+12+40=601\cdot 2 + 2\cdot 3 + 3\cdot 4 + 4\cdot 10 = 2+6+12+40 = 601‚ãÖ2+2‚ãÖ3+3‚ãÖ4+4‚ãÖ10=2+6+12+40=60. Since this value is even, this is not a lovely permutation of the array.
A lovely permutation of the given array is [3,1,10,2,4][3, 1, 10, 2, 4][3,1,10,2,4]. The sum of products for this array would be 3‚ãÖ1+1‚ãÖ10+10‚ãÖ2+2‚ãÖ4=413\cdot 1 + 1\cdot 10 + 10\cdot 2 + 2\cdot 4 = 413‚ãÖ1+1‚ãÖ10+10‚ãÖ2+2‚ãÖ4=41, which is odd.
Test Case 222: No permutation of the given array exists where the sum of products of adjacent pair of elements is odd. Thus, answer is ‚àí1-1‚àí1.
